items.title: Nieuwe Watford-manager Still moet evenaring van ontslagrecord zien te voorkomen
items.summary: Edward Still (rechts op de foto) is, zoals al in de media verscheen, aangesteld als nieuwe manager van Watford. De 35-jarige Brit verliet zondag zijn post als interim-trainer van het momenteel zeer roerige Anderlecht , maar zal op Vicarage Road ook niet in een oase van rust belanden.

items.title: Sources: Cristiano Ronaldo ends Al Nassr dispute
items.summary: Cristiano Ronaldo has decided to end his boycott of Al Nassr games, sources have told ESPN Brasil.

items.title: Laporta resigns as Barça president, will re-stand
items.summary: Joan Laporta has resigned as Barcelona president ahead of re-standing for the role when club members go to the polls on March 15.

items.title: Cazorla: 'LaLiga light years away from the PL'
items.summary: Santi Cazorla has taken a swipe at LaLiga after his team's league game at Rayo Vallecano on Saturday afternoon was postponed just hours before kickoff.

items.title: Foster: Wrexham's journey 'ridiculous'
items.summary: Ben Foster has hailed Wrexham's "ridiculous" journey as Hollywood owners Rob Mac and Ryan Reynolds celebrate their fifth anniversary in charge of the Welsh club.

items.title: Dankbare aanvoerder Nuytinck: 'Heb heel veel lieve berichten gekregen'
items.summary: Bram Nuytinck maakte zaterdag zijn rentree bij NEC, na bijna een jaar blessureleed. De verdediger kwam vlak voor tijd in het veld tijdens de ruime zege op Heracles Almelo. Na afloop stond Nuytinck Voetbal International te woord.

items.title: Forest latest: Dyche ignoring background noise in survival bid
Watford appoint Edward Still as head coach
items.summary: Watford appoint Edward Still as head coach following the resignation of Javi Gracia after only three months in charge.

items.title: Oefenduel Ajax: goal Zinchenko, minuten voor Berghuis
items.summary: Ajax heeft maandagmiddag een oefenwedstrijd gespeeld tegen Eredivisie-hekkensluiter Telstar. Het duel op De Toekomst werd met 2-0 gewonnen door Ajax, via goals van nieuweling Oleksandr Zinchenko en Kian Fitz-Jim. Steven Berghuis maakte weer eens minuten voor Ajax.

items.title: Sutton's predictions v golf superstar & Arsenal fan Ian Poulter
items.summary: BBC Sport football expert Chris Sutton takes on golf superstar Ian Poulter plus the BBC readers and AI with his predictions for this week's midweek Premier League fixtures.

items.title: Romero 'a leader but needs to learn' - Frank
items.summary: Tottenham captain Cristian Romero is a "leader" but has more to learn, manager Thomas Frank says following the defender's second sending-off of the season.

items.title: De Rossi witheet na VAR-moment: 'Welke sport is dit?'
items.summary: Het had niet veel gescheeld of Justin Bijlow had heel wat woede bij zijn coach kunnen voorkomen, maar de Nederlandse doelman van Genoa stopte de strafschop van Napoli net niet. Genoa-coach Daniele De Rossi hield zich dan ook niet in na afloop van het duel.

items.title: Portugese media: 'Ronaldo is uitgestaakt en gaat binnenkort weer voetballen'
items.summary: De staking van Cristiano Ronaldo lijkt voorbij. Portugese media melden maandag dat de aanvaller van Al-Nassr van plan is om zaterdag zijn kicksen weer aan te trekken.

items.title: Football Daily | VAR v vibes: there can be only one winner in the Premier League
items.summary: Sign up now! Sign up now! Sign up now? Sign up now! Perhaps the only thing everyone can agree on is that, according to our old friend The Letter of the Law, the “goal” Rayan Cherki scored from inside his own half in the dying moments of added time of Manchester City’s wild win at Liverpool was correctly ruled out. It took a while but, between them, referee Craig Pawson and his coterie of curtain-twitching buzz-killing fun sponges in the Stockley Park VAR bunker got there in the end. Football Daily shudders to think about how much tedious debate Cherki could have put a stop to by simply putting his boot through the ball a bit harder. But we’re guessing he spent much of his weekend watching curling at the Winter Olympics and was trying to channel his inner Jen Dodds by sending the ball trundling on its merry way with just enough force to cross the goal line but not so much that it might do anything so vulgar as ripple the net. Like the match officials, it got there in the end but only after Dominik Szoboszlai and Erling Haaland had engaged in some tit-for-tat fouling that led to the goal being correctly disallowed, the Hungarian correctly sent off and an entertaining debate between those who would prefer matches to be ultimately governed by “vibes” over VAR. Is Friday’s last line (full email edition) a link to Ian Holloway’s meltdown and Harry Enfield’s much-loved pub character Frank Doberman, whose pleasant mien gradually dissolves, finishing up in full-on rant mode? It was either that or the flat-capped ‘You don’t want to do it like that, did you?’ fella shaking his head at Jhon Durán’s latest career move to Russia” – Mike Kilner. This is an extract from our daily football email … Football Daily. To get the full version, just visit this page and follow the instructions. Continue reading...

items.title: You may not like the Liverpool red card, but it was the right call | Jonathan Wilson
items.summary: Referee Craig Pawson sent off Dominik Szoboszlai by the letter of the law; the only way it should be done Sign up for Soccer with Jonathan Wilson here Refereeing is the most thankless of jobs. There are times when you can get a decision absolutely right and still you get criticised on all sides. In the final seconds at Anfield on Sunday, with the Liverpool goalkeeper Alisson caught upfield, Rayan Cherki rolled the ball towards the Liverpool goal. Erling Haaland gave chase and would have gotten there to nudge the ball definitively over the line but he was pulled back by Dominik Szoboszlai, who would then have caught up with the ball to clear had he not been pulled back by Haaland. The ball crossed the line but the referee Craig Pawson, after a VAR review, gave not a goal but a free-kick for the first offence, sending Szoboszlai off for the denial of an obvious goal-scoring opportunity. Continue reading...

items.title: FIA want F1 engine controversy solved 'not in the courtroom'
items.summary: The FIA say they want to keep a dispute between Formula 1 teams over the sport's new 2026 power units out of "the courtroom" as they seek to solve the issue before the start of the new season.

items.title: Late goals are destroying Liverpool's season and the answers aren't obvious
items.summary: Why have Liverpool gone from mentality monsters to a side there for the taking?

items.title: 'Hell yeah!' - Kubiak confirms Raiders head coaching move after Super Bowl
items.summary: Seattle Seahawks offensive coordinator Klint Kubiak has essentially confirmed he will be the next Las Vegas Raiders head coach following his side's Super Bowl 60 win over the New England Patriots.

items.title: Maye had shoulder injection before three turnovers in Super Bowl loss
items.summary: New England Patriots quarterback Drake Maye revealed he received a pain-killing injection on his injured throwing shoulder before the team's 29-13 loss to the Seattle Seahawks in Super Bowl 60.

items.title: St Pauli plotting their next miracle in tantalising Bundesliga survival battle | Andy Brassell
items.summary: Win against Stuttgart was a reminder that unity remains St Pauli’s greatest strength in defying the odds again It had begun to look like a lost cause. In a season where the Bundesliga’s relegation battle increasingly promises a richness that the title race may lack (with all due respect to Borussia Dortmund’s efforts to stalk Bayern Munich at closer quarters in recent weeks), it has felt like St Pauli were, like fellow minnows Heidenheim, ready to be cut away. The Hamburg club’s best-ever start to a top-flight season, two wins and a draw from their first three games, felt like an age ago. Nine successive defeats will do that to you. Yet these masters of the unusual and the unexpected had another surprise up their sleeve this weekend; not least, one suspects, to themselves. Stuttgart travelled north on a fine run of form, sitting pretty in a Champions League spot and fresh from a week of qualifying for the DFB Pokal semi-finals, a trophy which they have every hope of retaining. With one league win against largely hopeless Heidenheim since that golden start for their hosts, who are also harbouring an injury list as long as one of Scottie Pippen’s arms (to paraphrase Jay-Z), it looked straightforward for Sebastian Hoeness and his men. Continue reading...
